title: Electricity generation from renewables
subtitle: Measured in [terawatt-hours](#dod:watt-hours). Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.
caption:
Electricity generation from renewables
Measured in terawatt-hours. Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.

The 2025 world map displays electricity generation from renewable sources across countries using a color scale ranging from 0 TWh to over 2,000 TWh. China and the United States occupy the darkest blue band, indicating generation levels exceeding 1,000 TWh, with China appearing in the deepest shade suggesting values approaching or surpassing the 2,000 TWh upper limit. Brazil, Canada, and India fall within the medium-to-dark blue bands, corresponding to ranges roughly between 200 TWh and 1,000 TWh. Russia and several European nations including Germany, France, and Spain sit in the lighter blue bands, spanning approximately 50 TWh to 200 TWh. Most countries in Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia are colored in pale green or off-white, placing them in the lowest bands between 0 TWh and 50 TWh. Greenland, Western Sahara, and a few small island states show hatching, denoting no available data. Australia, Mexico, and Argentina appear in light green shades, aligning with the 10 TWh to 50 TWh range. The gradient reveals substantial variation: while a handful of large economies dominate the highest bands, the majority of countries cluster in the lower end of the scale, with many falling below 20 TWh. No country outside East Asia and North America reaches the top two legend bands. The distribution is continuous, with intermediate shades visible across South America, Southeast Asia, and Eastern Europe, reflecting generation levels that span multiple adjacent bands rather than clustering at extremes.
